one_line_takeaway: Framing a harmful query as a multi-turn detective-story of AI-generated images plus a 'write it up as a report' twist jailbreaks six major MLLMs at up to 17.5% higher success than prior multimodal attacks, exposing that visual-modality and multi-turn context evade current safety alignment.
key_finding: MIRAGE achieves the highest ASR in almost every model/dataset cell, improving over the best baseline by up to 17.5%. On RedTeam-2K it reaches 63.67% on Gemini-1.5-Pro and 52.72% on Grok-2V; on GPT-4V it hits 43.13% vs Visual-RolePlay's 35.57% (+7.56pt). The single exception is Gemini-1.5-Pro on HarmBench, where MIRAGE (65.63%) trails HADES (68.43%). Ablations show multi-turn interaction is the most critical component (removing it drops ASR by up to ~17.3%). Three decomposition images are optimal: ASR rises from 12.3% (1 image) to 65.6% (3 images) then plateaus at 67.0% (4-5 images), with efficiency peaking at 0.027 ASR/token. The detective persona beats psychologist/historian/chemist/engineer. A lightweight BLIP-2 pre-screening defense cuts ASR by up to 15.67%.
core_contribution: MIRAGE is a fully black-box, narrative-driven multimodal jailbreak framework for Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). Its own novel contribution is a two-stage attack pipeline: (Stage I) a toxic query is decomposed into an environment-character-activity triplet and each element is converted into a separate Stable-Diffusion-generated image, then fed to the target model across multiple turns rather than all at once; (Stage II) the target model is guided to adopt a 'detective' persona (persona-based reverse reasoning) and to answer in a retrospective format (letter/report/investigation summary) that reframes the harmful request as a post-hoc account. The authors claim to be the first to apply narrative-structure and cognitive-load framing to multimodal jailbreaks, and identify that a task-oriented detective persona activates a model bias toward 'narrative completeness' that leads the model to spontaneously override its own safety constraints. Note: perturbation/image/text-based attack categories and the RLHF/keyword-filter defenses described in the Intro and Related Work are cited prior work, not MIRAGE's contribution.
threat_model: Black-box adversary with only API-level access to a target MLLM (no weights, no gradients, no logits). The attacker can send multi-turn image+text prompts. Attacker-controlled tooling (an auxiliary LLM, DeepSeek-Chat, plus Stable-Diffusion-XL) runs offline to build the decomposition and images. Goal: elicit harmful content (from 16 RedTeam-2K / 7 HarmBench harm categories, e.g. chemical/biological harm, malware, illegal activity, animal abuse) that the model would refuse if asked directly. The exploited weakness is the asymmetry that MLLMs police explicit textual toxicity more strongly than equivalent visual content, combined with multi-turn context accumulation lowering vigilance.
limitations: Success labeling leans on Perspective API (threshold 0.5), which the authors themselves note misreads sarcasm, implicit toxicity and context; human review only re-checks flagged (positive) responses, so false negatives are not audited and reported ASRs may be soft. Absolute ASRs remain modest (mostly 43-65%), i.e. most attacks still fail. The proposed defense is evaluated only on the three white-box open-source models on HarmBench, not on the commercial targets. The method is presented largely qualitatively with a single worked example (the 'poison my bird' animal-abuse query); no statistical significance testing beyond the image-count 'plateau' claim. The attack depends on an auxiliary LLM and SDXL, adding an offline pipeline dependency. A minor internal inconsistency: LLaVA-Mistral RedTeam-2K MIRAGE ASR is 52.15 in Table 1 but 52.12 in the ablation Table 2.

datasets_used: RedTeam-2K (2000 toxic queries, 16 categories from 8 sources)
quantitative_results: Headline: up to 17.5% ASR improvement over best baseline
RedTeam-2K MIRAGE ASR: LLaVA-Mistral 52.15%, Qwen-VL 45.02%, Intern-VL 44.23%, Gemini-1.5-Pro 63.67%, GPT-4V 43.13%, Grok-2V 52.72%
HarmBench MIRAGE ASR: LLaVA-Mistral 43.40%, Qwen-VL 47.24%, Intern-VL 50.60%, Gemini-1.5-Pro 65.63%, GPT-4V 44.65%, Grok-2V 48.91%
Only loss: Gemini-1.5-Pro on HarmBench MIRAGE 65.63% vs HADES 68.43% (-2.80pt)
Image-count sweep (Gemini/HarmBench): ASR 12.3% (1 img) -> 65.6% (3 img) -> 67.0% (4-5 img); efficiency peaks at 0.027 ASR/token at 3 images; ~800 tokens per added image
Ablation drops (largest = multi-turn removal): MT removal drops ASR up to 17.33% (LLaVA RedTeam-2K); VS/RI removals ~7-16%; adding textual storytelling reduces ASR ~7-14%
Defense: BLIP-2 pre-screening reduces MIRAGE ASR by 14.24% / 15.67% / 10.70% (LLaVA/Qwen/Intern on HarmBench); CLIP by 12.85% / 10.49% / 9.25%
Persona comparison: Detective highest ASR (e.g. Gemini 63.7%) vs Psychologist/Historian/Chemist/Engineer
Cost: ~8.21e-4 USD per query via DeepSeek-Chat; ~2.6e-3 to 4.1e-3 USD per full attack on Gemini-1.5-Pro; ~2100-3300 tokens per commercial-model attack
